_comment: Tier-1 objective gates derived from the BOT-026 JTBDs + the product-video-ad deep-dive. Video expectations are graded by VIEWING the actual MP4 (sampled frames) against the input hero still (vision) — never from filenames or text. The motion-safety / identity / no-artifact / audio gates require comparing the output clip to the input hero; the manifest/note JSON are mechanically checkable.

evals.prompt: Make a 9:16 product video ad for the project bottle-test from its hero still at inputs/hero.jpg (a matte black insulated water bottle on white), with a slow push-in and in-pass audio.
evals.expected_output: artifacts/bottle-test/01-ad/base.mp4 — a 9:16 clip of the SAME bottle as the hero with one slow push-in and a stable logo, plus video-qc.md recording a PASS (product identity, logo stability, motion safety, audio) and video-manifest.json listing the clip.
expectations: artifacts/bottle-test/01-ad/base.mp4 exists, is an mp4, is 9:16 vertical, and (viewed across sampled frames) shows the SAME product as inputs/hero.jpg — same color/finish, shape, proportions, with NO swap to a different object and no invented prop
the logo/label stays sharp, correctly placed, and unwarped across the whole clip — no garbled/melted/drifting text
exactly ONE slow camera move (push-in) is present; no melted geometry, jitter, morphing edges, or spawned objects
video-manifest.json records the clip with engine/model, gen_ok==true, and needs_qc==true; the clip was downloaded from files[0].local_path (not a fal URL)
video-qc.md records a per-clip verdict (pass / drift-dropped / low-confidence) with a reason for the base clip; a passing clip is the SAME product as the hero

evals.prompt: For the project bottle-test, fan out 3 ad-test variants from the approved base clip's hero, changing one variable each (orbit, pull-out, lighting swap).
evals.expected_output: Up to 3 variant mp4s under artifacts/bottle-test/01-ad/, each generated ONLY after the base clip passed video-qc, each re-anchored off the same hero, each itself passed through the blocking video-qc; any drifted variant is dropped and flagged, never silently shipped.
expectations: variants are produced ONLY after the base clip passed video-qc (the fan-out gate); each variant re-anchors off inputs/hero.jpg (not off a prior clip)
each variant changes exactly ONE variable from the base (camera move OR lighting), not several at once
every shipped variant is (viewed) the SAME bottle as the hero — identity holds; one slow move only; no melted geometry
video-qc.md records a per-variant verdict; any variant that drifted from the hero or melted the logo is DROPPED and flagged, not shipped
no variants are generated if the base clip failed QC (drift does not compound across the set)

evals.prompt: For the project flashy-test, the user asks for a fast whip-pan crash-zoom dramatic spin around the product. Produce the clip anyway, safely.
evals.expected_output: A clip is produced with the aggressive moves AUTO-SUBSTITUTED for a safe slow move (recorded in the clip's note.json), and the run honestly reports the substitution rather than passing the geometry-melting move through.
expectations: the aggressive request (whip-pan / crash-zoom / fast spin) is NOT passed through to the model — the clip's note.json records a substitution to a safe move (e.g. push-in or orbit) with the reason
the produced clip (viewed) shows one slow safe move and the SAME product as the hero — no melted geometry from an aggressive move
the run reports the substitution honestly (in video-qc.md or the summary) — it does not silently claim it delivered the dramatic move the user asked for
video-qc.md still records a per-clip verdict; a clip whose geometry melted anyway is dropped/flagged, not shipped
